behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure can occur at the git-command boundary (e.g., rename detection flags/format) plus parser/aggregator interaction, not only inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: A stubbed diff can falsely pass even if real git output reports delete+add because invocation/options are wrong; using a real repo and real git process is the reliable failure boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI/ML output and no agent pipeline. Integration-brake checks: (a) yes, real subprocess/repo state matters -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. Adversarial critique: if implementation consumes only an injected normalized change object, Integration could be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text says tool reads a git repository; correctness depends on real rename detection behavior from git, which Integration with canned output can miss. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is about git file-status categorization only, with no model call or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit baseline, perform a real `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report has rename count incremented while added/deleted counts do not include that same path pair.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test description explicitly requires real git subprocess + real repo state; a lower-level mock could miss invocation/format failures.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Crash risk emerges when real git emits binary-diff metadata and the CLI parses process output/end codes. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: Mocking diff text can hide encoding/format/exit-code behaviors that only appear with real binary content and real git subprocess I/O. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No model dependency or multi-agent chain. Integration-brake checks: (a) yes -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. Adversarial critique: if parser accepts a fixed UTF-8 safe fixture, Integration might catch parser bugs. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text explicitly targets binary diffs not crashing; that boundary is real subprocess output under real repo data, not only module wiring. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected since behavior text contains no AI/ML decision quality or staged agent handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit baseline, add/modify a binary file, run CLI subprocess against repo, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in the relevant category/count.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires real git + binary file handling path; lower-level fakes can miss real output/encoding edge cases.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git state plus subprocess exit/status handling issue, typically across command runner + error mapping modules. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: Stubs may not replicate exact no-commit git responses and exit codes, so false passes are likely. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI/ML or agent pipeline. Integration-brake checks: (a) yes -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. Adversarial critique: if git adapter already normalizes no-commit state into a deterministic in-process error type, Integration could suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text is explicitly about a repository state with no commits; real git process semantics for that state are the failure boundary. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output or sequential model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh repo with zero commits, execute CLI as subprocess, assert zero exit and an empty structured report instead of exception/error output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The scenario depends on real git repo state and command behavior; lower levels can miss exit-code/message handling regressions.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is end-to-end reconciliation against real repository history; failures can come from git query selection, revision range handling, and aggregation wiring together. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: Using canned histories can hide mismatches caused by actual git history traversal and subprocess output differences. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI/ML dependence. Integration-brake checks: (a) yes -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. Adversarial critique: for a narrowly scoped deterministic history fixture passed directly into aggregator, Integration could catch arithmetic bugs. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text says 'actual change history of the repository being analyzed'; that directly points to real git history interaction as minimum boundary. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected due absence of model-based judgment or agentic chaining in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with scripted commits containing known add/delete/rename/modify events, run CLI subprocess, and assert each reported count equals the known ground truth from that real history.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly validates against real repository history; lower-level tests with synthetic data can miss history-query regressions.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears in interaction between git index/worktree states and command selection logic; not just local pure logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: Mocking status/diff responses can miss real staged vs unstaged semantics from git and subprocess invocation flags. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI/ML content dependency. Integration-brake checks: (a) yes -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. Adversarial critique: if code only compares two already-fetched normalized change sets, Integration might be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text requires equivalence across real git states (staged vs unstaged), which is a real VCS/process boundary. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text concerns deterministic git state handling, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent file edits once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess for each state, and assert structured outputs are byte-for-byte identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real index/worktree transitions and real git behavior; in-process fakes can conceal state-semantic differences.
